title: Stranvaesia scandens
intro: Stranvaesia scandens, also known as Chinese angelica tree, is a climbing plant native to China and India. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include lignans, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Stranvaesia scandens is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.

Stranvaesia scandens is also classified as a member of the genus Stranvaesia, which is a small genus of flowering plants endemic to Asia.
